text: If the patient has an open fracture responders should :

options.text: Attempt to push bones back into the wound.
Use bulky dressings to pad around the protruding bones ends.
Apply pressure directly over the fracture to control bleeding.
Apply a tourniquet above the fracture site.

explanation: With an open fracture, the primary goal is to prevent further infection. Bulky dressings should be applied around the protrusion; the bone must never be forced back, nor should pressure be applied directly over the bone end.
